# libGDX Headless Polygon Geometry CLI

## Background

You are building a deterministic command-line geometry utility on top of libGDX's headless backend. The tool reads a small text-based scripting language describing 2D convex polygons and queries, then evaluates each query using libGDX math primitives (notably `com.badlogic.gdx.math.Intersector` and `com.badlogic.gdx.math.Polygon`) inside a `HeadlessApplication` and writes one result per query to standard output. No OpenGL context, no native windowing, and no audio must be initialized.

## Requirements

* Implement a Gradle-based libGDX project at `/home/user/myproject` that runs entirely with the headless backend (`com.badlogicgames.gdx:gdx-backend-headless:1.14.2`).
* Pin the libGDX version to `1.14.2` (core `com.badlogicgames.gdx:gdx:1.14.2`).
* Wire an `ApplicationListener` (or `ApplicationAdapter`) that runs under `new HeadlessApplication(listener, config)` with `config.updatesPerSecond = 0` so the program ticks once and exits.
* Read the path to the script file from the first program argument (forwarded via `--args`).
* For each non-empty, non-comment line in the script, execute exactly one of the commands below and print exactly one output line to stdout. Use libGDX's `Intersector` and `Polygon` classes for the geometric primitives.
* After processing every line, call `Gdx.app.exit()` and join the main loop thread cleanly.
* A `run.sh` wrapper script must be provided at the project root that accepts the script-file path as the first argument and forwards it to the headless launcher, writing only the program's stdout lines (no Gradle banner / progress output) to stdout.
* A line starting with `#` or a blank line is ignored.

### Script language

All coordinates are floating point numbers. Polygon names match `[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9_]*`. Polygons defined by the script are convex and given in counter-clockwise order.

* `POLY <name> <x1> <y1> <x2> <y2> ... <xn> <yn>` — store a polygon with the given vertex coordinates (at least 3 vertices, so at least 6 numbers).
  * Output: `POLY <name> <vertexCount>`
* `OVERLAP <a> <b>` — test whether convex polygons `<a>` and `<b>` overlap using `Intersector.overlapConvexPolygons`.
  * Output: `OVERLAP <a> <b> true` or `OVERLAP <a> <b> false`
* `CONTAINS <name> <x> <y>` — test whether point `(x, y)` is inside polygon `<name>` using `Intersector.isPointInPolygon` on the stored vertex array.
  * Output: `CONTAINS <name> <x> <y> true` or `CONTAINS <name> <x> <y> false`
  * Print the input coordinates back exactly as they appeared in the script line (preserve the formatting tokens the user typed).
* `SEGMENTS <ax> <ay> <bx> <by> <cx> <cy> <dx> <dy>` — intersect segment `AB` with segment `CD` using `Intersector.intersectSegments(Vector2, Vector2, Vector2, Vector2, Vector2)`.
  * If the segments meet, output: `SEGMENTS hit <px> <py>` where `<px>` and `<py>` are the intersection point coordinates rounded to **three decimal places** using `Locale.ROOT` (use `String.format(Locale.ROOT, "%.3f", value)`).
  * Otherwise, output: `SEGMENTS miss`
* `AREA <name>` — compute the signed area of polygon `<name>` via `Polygon.area()`.
  * Output: `AREA <name> <value>` where `<value>` is rounded to three decimal places using `Locale.ROOT`.

An unknown command, a reference to an undefined polygon, or a malformed numeric token must cause the program to print `ERROR <line-number> <command-or-reason>` (1-based line number counting blank and comment lines) and continue to the next line.

## Implementation Hints

* The `gdx-backend-headless` artifact pulls in the desktop natives loader, so a plain Gradle `application` plugin run is sufficient — no LWJGL window is required.
* `Intersector.overlapConvexPolygons` accepts `com.badlogic.gdx.math.Polygon` instances. `Intersector.isPointInPolygon` takes a `float[]` (or `(float[] polygon, int offset, int count, float x, float y)`).
* `HeadlessApplicationConfiguration.updatesPerSecond = 0` runs the loop as fast as possible; you can do all your work in `create()` and then call `Gdx.app.exit()`.
* Read the script through `Gdx.files.absolute(path)` or plain `java.nio.file.Files` — both work under the headless `Files` implementation.
* Make sure your `run.sh` filters out Gradle's own logging (e.g. use `./gradlew -q` or capture only the launcher's stdout) so that the only lines emitted are the result lines defined above.
* The headless launcher runs the `ApplicationListener` on its own thread; wait for the main loop to finish before exiting `main()` so all output is flushed.

## Acceptance Criteria

* Project path: /home/user/myproject
* Command: `bash run.sh <input-script-path>`
* Input argument format: `<input-script-path>` is the absolute path to a UTF-8 text file that conforms to the script language above.
* The stdout must contain exactly one result line per non-empty, non-comment input line, in the order the commands appear.
* All result lines must follow the exact formats listed in Requirements (including the literal command keyword in upper-case).
* The program must exit with status code 0 for any well-formed script.
* The implementation must use `com.badlogicgames.gdx:gdx-backend-headless:1.14.2` and `com.badlogicgames.gdx:gdx:1.14.2`, and must run under `HeadlessApplication`.
* Floating-point output must use `Locale.ROOT` and `%.3f` (three fractional digits, `.` as the decimal separator).
* `run.sh` must not print any output other than the program's stdout lines (Gradle banners, daemon notices, and progress logs must be suppressed).
* The build must be reproducible offline once dependencies are resolved on the first run (subsequent invocations must not require network access).
